title: Biotech’s digital twin dilemma - Nature Biotechnology
gist: Digital twins - virtual patients, simulated cells, and organs-on-chips - are increasingly used to forecast disease and guide drug development, but the field's central unresolved dilemma is how to define, validate, and deploy these models.

insights.claim: Digital twins spanning virtual patients, simulated cells, and organs-on-chips are being used to forecast disease progression and guide drug development.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: Flags an emerging simulation layer in pharma R&D - a growth vector for computational biology and in-silico trial tooling vendors.
insights.quote: digital twins are forecasting disease and guiding drug development.

insights.claim: The unresolved bottleneck for biotech digital twins is that scientists must still figure out how to define, validate, and deploy these models.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: Validation and standardization - not model-building - is the gating hurdle; whoever solves it (standards bodies, regulators, tool vendors) captures the value.
insights.quote: Scientists now must figure out how to define, validate and deploy these models.
